# Validation of the MOBQ-12 Mobility Questionnaire in Adults with Lower Limb Amputation

## Abstract
Objective: This study aimed to validate the MOBQ-12 mobility questionnaire in adults with lower limb amputation.
Methods: In this prospective cross-sectional validation study, participants completed the MOBQ-12 at baseline and again after two weeks to evaluate test-retest reliability.
Hypothesis testing for construct validity was performed by comparing MOBQ-12 scores to FAST-WALK scores and BAL-INDEX scores.
FAST-WALK is a 10-meter timed walk test that reports performance time in seconds.
BAL-INDEX is a balance confidence scale completed by participants.
FAST-WALK scores were recorded at baseline and follow-up.
Results: MOBQ-12 showed good test-retest reliability (ICC = 0.88). Correlation with FAST-WALK was r = -0.62 and correlation with BAL-INDEX was r = 0.66.

## Methods
The target population was adults with lower limb amputation recruited from an outpatient rehabilitation clinic.
A total of 52 participants were enrolled in the validation sample.
No gold standard was available, so criterion validity was not assessed in this study.
